sources.exact_document_title: Guidance for Professional Practice Annex 4: Urgency of referrals table
sources.population: People with eye presentations requiring emergency, urgent, or priority referral triage.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for listed eye red flags and urgency categories; local UAE referral pathways and timing require clinician review.

exact_sections.heading: Annex 4 — anterior eye and traumatic red-eye referrals
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

exact_sections.heading: Annex 4 — visual loss and other urgent presentations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

archive.title: Guidance for Professional Practice Annex 4: Urgency of referrals table
archive.population: People with eye presentations requiring emergency, urgent, or priority referral triage.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Annex 4 — anterior eye and traumatic red-eye referrals
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Lists severe chemical burns, penetrating injuries, hyphema, embedded corneal foreign body, corneal or lid laceration, blunt trauma, severe abrasion, infective keratitis, and other urgent anterior-eye presentations.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Annex 4 — visual loss and other urgent presentations
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Lists sudden visual loss, sudden vision change, flashes or floaters with retinal signs, retinal detachment, acute diplopia, severe eye pain with nausea or vomiting, pain on eye movement, and new pupil defects.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Headaches in over 12s: diagnosis and management
sources.population: People aged 12 years and over with headache disorders, including migraine.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist assessment and follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for headache and migraine assessment in people aged 12 years and over; partial for adult-only DHA comparison and not directly validated for UAE pathways outside the DHA source.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — assessment, red flags, and headache diary
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sudden onset, neurological deficit, fever, trauma, pattern change, unexplained vomiting, further investigation or referral, and structured headache-diary fields.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — headache and migraine features
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports headache location, quality, severity, duration, frequency, activity impact, nausea, vomiting, light or sound sensitivity, aura, menstrual relation, and medication-overuse review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.6 — diary, reassessment, referral, and information
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording headache frequency, duration, severity, intervention effectiveness, impact, and clinician-led further investigation or referral when red flags develop.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.10–1.3.33 — migraine treatment and prophylaxis review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documentation that an acute or preventive migraine plan was clinician-decided and that prophylaxis was reviewed; no medicine name or dose is mapped.
